rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior, and assignments in implementation scopes.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers and named constants to define bounds and logical relationships in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` declarations within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly unless represented via variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific assignments in component type scopes.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
